title: Plectronia hispida
taxonomy: Plectronia hispida is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of approximately 600 genera and over 13,000 species. Plectronia hispida is further classified within the subfamily Rubioideae, a subfamily within the family Rubiaceae. The genus Plectronia is native to tropical Africa, and Plectronia hispida is one of the species within this genus. The exact classification of Plectronia hispida is further refined within the family Rubiaceae.

intro: Plectronia hispida, also known as the hairy plectronia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub native to tropical Africa, used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to exhibit potential health benefits in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Plectronia hispida include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Plectronia hispida is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, and is also used as an ingredient in traditional remedies for fever, cough, and skin conditions.
